heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 18 July 2026 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 2nd since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 19 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 03 July 2026.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 3 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 27.7°C on 24 April 2026.  On that date the temperature reached 23.2°C and fell to -4.5°C.

heatwaves.status: You are currently experiencing a heatwave that started on 2026-07-01 and so far it has lasted for 21 days.  This year there has been 3 heatwaves, the average number is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and it has only happened 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (218°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 2.5°C) and outside temperature (up 1.7°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 7%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 944 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 211 m) and light levels (down 4109 lux) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 944.5m lower than yesterday, 211.5m lower than the past week, 483.4m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1974m minimum and 8460m maximum, with an average of 4240m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 4450m and 3760m.
cloud levels.summary: A high cloud base will mean mostly sunny conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; heat will be very noticeable, though not extreme. Also, neither too dry nor too humid and very little to no wind will be present. A few light raindrops may fall, a high cloud base will mean mostly sunny conditions, and strong and powerful sunlight. No significant shifts in the weather are expected.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
